task_completion.description: Verify the agent logged into OpenProject using admin credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenProject web interface is visible, showing the user is logged in (e.g., user avatar in the top right, access to the Administration menu).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the password policy settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser displays the OpenProject Administration settings page specifically for Authentication or Password policies.

task_completion.description: Verify the minimum password length is set to 12.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The input field for 'Minimum password length' (or similar wording) contains the number '12'.

task_completion.description: Verify the policy requires uppercase letters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox or toggle corresponding to 'Uppercase letters' (or 'uppercase') in the password rules section is checked/enabled.

task_completion.description: Verify the policy requires lowercase letters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox or toggle corresponding to 'Lowercase letters' (or 'lowercase') in the password rules section is checked/enabled.
The checkbox or toggle corresponding to 'Numbers' (or 'numeric') in the password rules section is checked/enabled.

task_completion.description: Verify the new password policy settings were saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success notification (e.g., a green banner stating 'Successful update' or 'Settings saved') is visible, or the save button was clicked and the configured values persist on the screen.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web browser GUI to configure the settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a web browser interacting with the OpenProject interface, rather than terminal commands (like curl or rails console) being used to change the database directly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual OpenProject application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows the correct local or remote address for OpenProject, and the interface looks like genuine software rather than a mocked text file or edited HTML.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the visual state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) are not open and being used to artificially inject the number '12' or check the boxes without actually saving the configuration.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires setting the OpenProject password policy to a minimum length of 12 and requiring uppercase, lowercase, and numeric characters. Review of the OpenProject source code confirms that the system internally represents these requirements using the exact strings 'uppercase', 'lowercase', and 'numeric'. Look for these specific character class options being enabled in the UI.
